column23: Comments

column2: Maximus the Mad's Inescapable "Kyln" Space Prison
column23: Once I had enough attack cards, purposely failed the scheme. This gave Maximus no abomination.  Then just hit him over and over again.

This setup was a blast. Warlock, Gamora, and Rocket&Groot pair well with one another. Mantis and Karma play great support roles and the Yaka Arrow made Karma even more of a powerhouse. Besides the one escape and not being as fast as I would have liked, I'm very proud of this score.

Crystal and Queen Storm make a great team! My first run brought on a lot of early scheme twists. That made it extremely difficult to get enough attack to hit Maximus. Second run was pretty evenly matched with henchmen and other villians during the first turns. Abomination and empowered on villians were amazingly helpful throughout with this scheme.

Due to Schrodinger's Cat shenanigans, my wife was able to pick up Sunspot's rare for free as a result of fighting Lash. Due to the scheme effects that card was pretty much always an 8 attack card. Shredded Maximus to bits quite easily after acquiring that!!

The Mastermind,  villain groups and scheme combo ended up making for a very interesting game overall.  Our second game we didn't get near as many twists/strikes up front which really helped make some dynamic decks. Mantis rare was super clutch for us
The facedown heroes in the HQ made the Abominations easier to fight than usual.

This game was a slog. I was keeping the imprisoned cards face down to keep Maximus at 8 hit points...I missed out but 1 hit point the last 3 turns and couldn't land final blow...last card in villian deck was scheme twist that ended game...my first play was a bloodbath and ended quickly.
You'd think this would nerf the MM and his VG, but it makes making efficient decks near impossible.

Wolverine/Angel drawing cards on once hand with Colossus/Hulk smashing wounds on the other was fun. Happy to have Magneto give me Woverine's rare for free, and then give it to me two turns in a row later on.

Cyc and Wolvie seemed like solid choices to have multiple ways to mitigate those wounds. And it worked quite well. Left hand was a Cyc heavy deck that just did what he does. Right was Colossus and Hulk pounding the crap out of everything. Wolvie supported everywhere. Hawkeye was… there.

Right side got early Versatile and Nightcrawler's red and uncommon, activating the uncommon almost every shuffle (also thanks to an early Juggernaut Ambush). No sidekicks makes me waste a lot of money late game lol. Left went Hulk/Hawkeye, worked alright. Didn't see a single green card all game. Only 2 bystanders KO'd.

Game 2 went... poorly. Hulk and Colossus showed up just enough to give out a bunch of wounds. Drew a lot more bystanders and fewer Instinct heroes.

The setup was fine, I decided that one X-Men hero would be enough (especially since he Teleports), and discarding down to 4 isn't that bad. Facing Magneto made me opt for a fast game, which I enjoyed.
Got lucky with scheme twists
Magneto seems weak, but you really need X-men to face him

Nothing to special in game one, had some nice Hulk and colloussus strength doing most of the work, wolverines were hiding for the most part. Second game had a promising start, but the hq bricked with 2 rares, a 6 cost, and 2 five costs fairly soon. Caused an escape, and overall did not go nearly as well. Setup is alright, some nice classic legendary, but nothing super fun or crazy

Between the bystanders in the hero deck because of the scheme and Hawkeye’s impossible trick shot bonus, I ran out of bystanders. Would have had at least another six bystanders rescued. Some fun combos and use of wounds to power up my heroes.

I didnt realize that I didn't pick an XMEN until after I started. That said, it was completely fine because I didn't run into Blob and most times when it would give me a wound, Colossus was there to help or Hulk would KO it. Great game! Everyone worked well together. Forge and Hawkeye worked well together, and Colossus and Hulk worked well together too!

Oddly, I had just done this setup in my most recent Arcade run. Interesting that it can be played conservatively (never spending below 2 recruit so as not to leave bystanders in the HQ) or not (spending recruit as normal). I did slightly better playing with more risk, but that could have been sheer luck!

column2: Bastion Bathes Earth in Cosmic Rays
column23: Feast side feasted once. Hiroim side lost ramp to the scheme. Somehow it worked out anyway.
First time I lost second tie I was able to thin my left hand to a deck of seven cards that consistently yielded a minimum of 10 attack. Bastion didn’t stand a chance.
Mr. Fantastic Giant Hand + Amadeus Cho and his rare did all the work. Not too bad!
In retrospect it was a very dangerous game choosing No Name to go with Miek but thankfully the feasting is optional so I didn’t worry about trying to transform them at all. Thanks for the tournament!

Getting 2 copycat bystanders in the first time you fight bastion followed immediately on the next turn by a cloning sentinel squadron is not good for escapes. My other game took a few turns longer and had less vps but 0 escapes. Let's hope I chose to submit the right one.

I underestimated how good Shatterstar is. Star Lord was a given that he was good, loved these decks! Early twists with no non greys made it a lot easier. Only feasted when known the top card was a grey card though.
We leaned into artifacts with the infinity gems to positive effect.
not a fan of the cloning mechanism, and there was just so much of it, managed to clear the masterminds on the final turn
OMG! Bastion is a beast with all that new and new masterminds... Rictor works very well with Shatterstar and even Miek or Drax can get something from their synergy. Close call but win.

column2: The Grim Reaper's Master of Tyrants
column23: I chose Lady Thor and Captain Marvel to go with Ms. America, letting the other 2 heroes be there as support. It went well in the first game, but both hands had similar decks and sometimes took cards that would have been better for the other hand. The second game was 2 turns longer, but no escape and way more VP so I went with that one. Decks were pretty similar to the first game, but with more presence with Squirrel Girl and Darkhawk since their cards came up earlier.
Master of Tyrants scheme is a little boring, but the rest of the setup was fun.
Played with my son and we also crushed it in the second game! Had a blast! Thank you for setting this up!
Had some good synergy with Captain Marvel and Ms. America on the left and Proxima  Midnight and Darkhawk on the right. I think my decks got too huge though. Not enough KO power.

Twist #1: Whiffed
Twist #2-7: All on the same Tyrant Villain

Great team and not a terrible scheme made for a really fun setup. Game 2, Rolling got to use Darkhawk's superpower on his rare and another Hyperspeed for a whooping Hyperspeed 13, counting both recruit and fight.
thanks for this opportunity! I hope i'm not already out!

It was a fun and challenging setup! I was on 4 out of 5 Guardians Defeated when I destroyed the city. Valkyrie was exeptionally good in this game combined with the Warriors Three. Right Hand used her Rare multiple times to deliver serious damage, while Left hand with only 14 cards in the deck by the end of the game utilized her thrown artifact combo with Draw card engine. In their respective final rounds Right had gathered 30 recruit and 22 attack (thanks to excessive kindness combo of Mantis and Groot) and Left side had managed to get 42 attack which was an overkill on Moloid and a 4 attack Ego! Fenomenal game really!
Our first play through had a significantly higher PPT, but it was around 7 or 8 turns higher.  We opted to submit the lower turn count in hopes it's good enough.
All bystanders except Damage Control (which was still in the villian deck) was rescued thanks mainly to Invisible Woman. I forgot how much fun Ego is as a mastermind. Won both attempts.
Moon Knight and Black Cat ended up being the winners of this set up!
1st game was the better game. I was able to get all three Big Hands in my deck and focused the recruit to have extra cards and the final time 3 times I hit Ego I had over 40 attack points!

I got a LOT of KO early on. The Hydra deck was drawing its full deck every turn, and the S.H.I.E.L.D. deck was drawing its full deck about every other turn. Once I got going the game ended very quickly. Second game didn't go so well, but I still had fun! Ego was very interesting and I look forward to playing against him again in the future!

I did not enjoy missing my sidekicks, special officers, special bystanders and even grievous wounds. Dismissing that, the setup was alright. I did not get any points from defeating Ego, although he was defeated 8 times. Feast and Doombot Legion took some pretty heavy hits on both decks, but victory was ensured with no escapes. Last turn (29) was a crazy 20 attack from two mjolnir's both being thrown two times and an also twice thrown stormbreaker for an even more impressive 24 attack (5 discarded cards for the first throw, 7 for the second). With 12 attack leftover from other cards as well it was pure overkill. The city was already completely empty and Ego just needed 6, 5 and a final 4 attack to die. The one and only guardian that did not defeat the villains in the City was Balder. It was kind of nice to have that many scheme twists that did not negatively impact the game. On the other hand, there weren't too many points to score because of that too, but the game was relatively short.

Deck building wise: Hand 1 went for Mr. Fantastic support cards (draw 2 cards x 2 and 'focus: draw an extra card' x 1) in the beginning. Later added two Mjolnirs and a few Beta Ray Bill commons. Eventually managed to get Beta Ray Bill's rare and a Stormbreaker. Lost its only uncommon Thor to Demogoblin's Feast, which hurt at the time, as it made Mjolnir loads better and with lots of other worthy cards that drew cards it was a powerhouse on its own.

Hand 2 went for Selfless/Selfish as a support strategy, but ended up playing it as Selfish instead of Selfless more often. It dIdn't really matter as some early master strikes added 2 wounds to both Hands. Bridqe Conqueror was only nice in the early game, later on nothing reached as far as even the Rooftops. Warrior's Three uncommon was part of the attack cards, and this deck also had a few Beta Ray Bill commons and finally added one Stormbreaker. I don't think it was used effectively though in this deck. 

Overall, having Ego (or Ronan the Accuser) as a Mastermind is not among my favourite Masterminds, because you score so incredibly few points. Even if I had hit 6 bystanders (which could perhaps be expected inn 8 defeats), the scores would not have been great. I would partly for that reason not want to play this setup again. It's also shocking how much I missed the special bystanders, officers and sidekicks. It made a huge difference. The Officers might as well not have been there at all, as I bought none and the sidekicks were missed dearly.
Interesting set up

column2: Thanos Weaves a Web of Lies
column23: Spidey deck to the left and iceman deck to the right, it was a great game with so many fun keywords. Nerkodd helped the right hand side a little.

first game gave me a lot of knowledge on what to do on the retry , the 2nd try i got so much value running a ice man/star lord artifacts side, my picks where based on cycling through the hq to the ice man/ranged cards with professor x and star lord provided great artifact synergy's against thanos. it worked out wonderfully while i must admit the first try was much less amazing.

I went with some heroes to try to help Iceman hit his big ramp, he barely bothered to show up either game unfortunately. Symbiote Spider-man was all over the hq so a lot of his cards ended up in the bound souls pile or KO'd to Phalanx because I didn't add any tech. My first game I didn't see any gems until near the end so I barely won. The second game went a little better with 5 gems captured for most of the game. Jean Grey helped a lot to not have to worry about the scheme. But Bishop was great in giving KO power and extra recruit from Phalanx KOs.

Skirn and Spidey was just overkill haha and I managed to get all 3 of the Shadowed Spiders counting my New Recruits each turn! Queen pulled a Gamora game with e of the Most Dangerous Woman in the Universe plus 90% of the Gems lol
Both games had very similar final stats with just a few differences
It is very easy to forget to buy bystanders for Web of Lies. I was super close to winning too. Just 1 tactic and final blow left. Star-lord just didn't show up like he should have. R.I.P. in peace

My first three turns were Time Gem into Space Gem, Shreik, Shreik.  All four of my escapes happened before turn 9.  Got a Iceman/Bishop deck online and was set to hit Thanos three times in one turn to finish the game but his Centuries of Envy tactic came up and I had to discard a gem so his fight went from 12 to 14 and caused me to fall two attack short of ending the game.  Next turn was the seventh twist.

First game turned up a starting HQ of 5, 5, 6, 6, and 7 and after not being able to recruit a *single* card in the HW by turn 8, I threw in the towel hoping for better days in the second attempt. 
(foreshadowing)
In Game 2, despite both decks generating between 30 and 60 attack in seven turns in a row(!) at the end of the game, the city filled up early and the Twists and strikes came late, in an exact order to be always one behind the Scheme to not allow me to touch Thanos even once. Off to the lower bracket we go.

Both games I played went well. Hand 1 mainly used Mockingbird and Wolverine to keep deck size as small as possible and get SHIELD level high (ended at 28). Hand 2 focused on clearing the city and using Phyla-Vell and Angel to gain shards and cycle through deck quickly. 

I apoligize again for messing up the first time, but more Legendary is always fun!
Pretty easy to not lose if you're paying attention, so I guess it comes down to speed
Hypersonic Scream + Reckless Abandon for the win!!!

Took Proxima to give Black Bolt more text less cards to work with. Domino tagged along, as her X Force affiliation, made her pair well with Wolverine, her cards with both attack and recruit would fuel Proxima, and she was in able to make use of Angel's discard shenanigans!

Punisher and Deathlok had some legendary deck thinning going on! Wolvie and Angel really struggled to get a draw/discard thing going in my 2nd game. I had so many wounds without a MK hero on one side, that'll probably be my downfall with turn count.

This setup felt like the right amount of difficulty and was a lot of fun. I thought I could just ignore wounds but that was a big mistake. In hindsight, I would have selected a hero that benefits from wounds or a Marvel Knight instead of Magik. Magik was good for triggering Iceman and getting Sidekick draw for Wolverine but she wasn't necessary.

Interesting game. I decided to focus on the drawing engine, so I added Magik to get access to more Sidekicks and Elektra as the MK hero to offset Mephisto's Master Strike since she had a bit of drawing power. Unfortunately, there wasn't a lot of deck thinning ability, so I think I only got rid of 1 starter card for the entirety of the game. That definitely prolonged the game and my ability to create bigger hands. In hindsight, I feel like maybe I should've chosen The Punisher as my MK hero for his deck thinning abilities but alas. The shards from the UCoT helped a lot. It took a while, but I was finally able to build a big hand on the last turn using Wolverine's Reckless Abandon card which resulted in drawing 12 cards for the entire hand and building up to 25 attack.

Game was tough as it felt like a war of attrition, but I got there in the end I got there. It did feel fairly on brand as Mephisto is a sower of discord, so seeing him be the puppet master of a S.H.I.E.L.D vs. Hydra war makes sense. Not sure I'd want to play this scenario again, but it was a good, tough first round setup.
Hard…

column2: Kang Floods the Planet with Melted Glaciers
column23: My brother went for Black Widow, Invisible women immediately and killed alot of Doombot Legions allowing him to clear out the base boys. So he racked up enough bystanders to hit Kang twice before he even got his second time incursion. 2 scheme twists came out immediately so we thought we were in trouble but it worked out in the end.

I wouldn't say there was anything super special about this setup, second run had more even point distribution, but also several additional turns taken. Still not sure what here combos well with gambit and wolverine, storm was a bit of a disappointment.
Tense at first, however got lucky with  the Skrulls nabbing the ultra rare cards so close together.
I am Annie's,d out...
Finally got to put Psi-Lord to the test and his ability to manage the villain deck was a game changer.
This one was really fun! I always love a chance to use FF cards.

Welp, we feel great about the one game we played! The fact that a lot of our scheme twists came out early helped clear some mediocre cards from the HQ and unearthed some rares. WaspWoman went big with a tech deck of Stark and the Richards gang with a side of Iron Lad, and AntDude managed to scrape together enough attack with Gambit and Wolvie to feel like he was contributing. Fun set up and I credit WaspWoman’s interest in geology as a factor that help us handle the melting glaciers.
Second run got lucky with 4 twists on the bottom of the VD.
Got Very Lucky (Legendary) First 3 Scheme Twists whiffed - 0 Heroes KO'ed
